 {"id":"bd-zrxb","title":"Task: Add ntm history command","description":"## Purpose
Provide CLI access to prompt history outside the TUI.

## Scope
- Add history.go to internal/cli/
- Commands: ntm history, ntm history search <query>, ntm history replay <id>
- Support JSON output for scripting

## Acceptance Criteria
- ntm history shows recent prompts
- Search works with regex and date filters
- Replay works from command line
- JSON output suitable for scripting

## Technical Notes
- Follow existing CLI patterns
- Integrate with robot mode for automation","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

-{"id":"br-04qv0","title":"Attention Feed Phase 1c: Derive the first-class attention signals an operator agent actually cares about","description":"# Why this bead exists
A raw event feed alone is still too noisy for an operator agent. What matters is not every underlying change, but the first-class attention signals that tell the LLM whether judgment is needed now. This bead adds those derived signals without pretending to become the decision-maker.

# Mandatory v1 signal set
- `session_changed`
- `pane_changed`
- `agent_state_changed`
- `stalled`
- `context_hot`
- `rate_limited`
- `alert_raised`

# Scope
1. Derive operator-relevant signals from existing lifecycle, activity, context, and alert sources.
2. Set actionability/severity for each signal class so the system can distinguish passive updates from real attention demands.
3. Attach machine-usable details such as pane indices, idle durations, context ratios, or alert IDs.
4. Emit follow-up suggestions that point operators toward existing targeted commands rather than giant snapshots unless resync is actually needed.
5. Keep thresholds and heuristics explainable and testable; if a stall or rate-limit rule exists, it must be obvious where it comes from.
6. Record threshold rationale in comments/tests so future tuning does not become folklore.

# Guardrails
- This bead must not treat raw output volume as equivalent to importance.
- A signal can be `interesting` without being `action_required`.
- If a threshold is heuristic, say so in code comments/tests rather than burying it.

# Detailed verification expectations
- Synthetic timeline tests for stall detection.
- Context-pressure tests around threshold boundaries.
- Tests proving passive output churn does not automatically become `action_required`.
- Scenario fixtures for lifecycle transitions, rate-limit text detection, and alert promotion.
- Debug logging in tests that prints the exact reason a signal was emitted, suppressed, or downgraded.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] The feed can wake an operator for real changes such as stalls, context pressure, or rate limits.
- [ ] Actionability classes are applied consistently across the mandatory v1 signal set.
- [ ] Signal payloads contain enough structured detail for precise follow-up.
- [ ] The implementation remains explainable rather than magical.
- [ ] Threshold rationale is visible to future maintainers.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:19.839224512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T03:27:02.483205212Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention-feed","phase-1","robot","signals"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-04qv0","depends_on_id":"br-7v4ns","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:22.870372511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-08fgf","title":"Phase 1a: Enable Mouse Support in Dashboard, Palette, and Tutorial","description":"# Enable Mouse Support Across All TUI Programs

## Background
NTM has 3 Bubble Tea programs (dashboard, palette, tutorial) that all use tea.WithAltScreen()
but NONE use tea.WithMouseCellMotion(). This is the single lowest-effort, highest-impact change
in the entire upgrade. Modern terminals (iTerm2, Ghostty, WezTerm, Windows Terminal, Kitty)
all support mouse events, and Bubble Tea handles them automatically.

## Current State (dashboard.go:7008)
  p := tea.NewProgram(model, tea.WithAltScreen())

## Target State
  p := tea.NewProgram(model, tea.WithAltScreen(), tea.WithMouseCellMotion())

## What tea.WithMouseCellMotion() Enables
- tea.MouseMsg events in Update() with position (X, Y), button, action (press/release/motion)
- Mouse wheel events (tea.MouseWheelUp, tea.MouseWheelDown)
- Click events for interactive elements
- Motion tracking for hover effects (cell-level granularity)

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Add mouse option to all 3 TUI programs:
- internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go:7008 (RunWithOptions)
- internal/cli/palette.go:112
- internal/cli/tutorial.go:60

### 2. Add tea.MouseMsg handler in dashboard Update():
- Click on pane list items: translate Y position to list index, select that pane
- Click on tab bar items: switch to that panel focus
- Mouse wheel in pane detail: scroll content up/down
- Click on toast: dismiss it
- Log mouse events at debug level: log.Printf("mouse: button=%d x=%d y=%d action=%d", ...)

### 3. Add tea.MouseMsg handler in palette Update():
- Click on command list items: select that command
- Mouse wheel: scroll command list

### 4. Add NTM_MOUSE env var control:
  if v, ok := os.LookupEnv("NTM_MOUSE"); ok && v == "0" {
      // Don't add WithMouseCellMotion — user opted out
  }
This allows disabling mouse if it conflicts with tmux mouse mode.

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)

### Test file: internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard_mouse_test.go
  func TestMouseClickSelectsPane(t *testing.T) {
      m := newTestModel(3) // 3 panes
      // Simulate mouse click at Y position of pane 2
      m, _ = m.Update(tea.MouseMsg{X: 5, Y: 8, Button: tea.MouseButtonLeft, Action: tea.MouseActionPress})
      if m.(Model).selectedPane != 1 { t.Errorf("expected pane 1 selected, got %d", m.(Model).selectedPane) }
  }

  func TestMouseWheelScrollsDetail(t *testing.T) {
      m := newTestModel(3)
      // Focus on detail pane, scroll down
      m, _ = m.Update(tea.MouseMsg{Button: tea.MouseButtonWheelDown})
      // Verify scroll position changed
  }

  func TestMouseDisabledByEnvVar(t *testing.T) {
      t.Setenv("NTM_MOUSE", "0")
      // Verify mouse option not added
  }

### Test file: internal/cli/palette_mouse_test.go
  func TestPaletteMouseClick(t *testing.T) { ... }

## E2E / VHS Verification
Create testdata/vhs/dashboard-mouse.tape:
  Output testdata/vhs/output/dashboard-mouse.gif
  Set FontSize 16
  Set Width 1800
  Set Height 1000
  Require ntm
  Hide
  Type "ntm dashboard test"
  Enter
  Show
  Sleep 3s
  # Mouse events are complex in VHS; verify via unit tests instead
  Type "q"

Run TUI Inspector: capture_ntm_tui.sh --profile ntm-dashboard-navigate
Compare snapshot to Phase 0 baseline to ensure no visual regression.

## Logging
- Debug log all mouse events when NTM_DEBUG=1: coordinates, button, action
- Log when mouse click hits a recognized target vs empty space
- Log when mouse mode is disabled by NTM_MOUSE=0

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tea.WithMouseCellMotion() added to all 3 TUI programs
- [ ] Mouse click selects panes in dashboard pane list
- [ ] Mouse wheel scrolls in focused panels
- [ ] NTM_MOUSE=0 disables mouse support
- [ ] All new unit tests pass: go test -v -run TestMouse ./internal/tui/dashboard/
- [ ] All existing tests still pass: go test -short ./internal/tui/...
- [ ] No visual regression in VHS captures
- [ ] Mouse events don't interfere with tmux mouse mode (manual test)

## Files to Modify
- internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go (line 7008 + MouseMsg handler in Update)
- internal/cli/palette.go (line 112)
- internal/cli/tutorial.go (line 60)

## Files to Create
None — add mouse tests to existing dashboard_render_pure_test.go
(AGENTS.md: no file proliferation for tests that belong in existing files)

## Risk: LOW — Adding WithMouseCellMotion is additive. Unhandled mouse events are ignored.
NTM_MOUSE=0 escape hatch for tmux conflicts.

## PARALLEL EXECUTION NOTE
This bead and Phase 1b (br-h3z1b) both modify dashboard.go. Changes are in
NON-OVERLAPPING sections (1a: RunWithOptions + MouseMsg case; 1b: DashboardTickMsg
handler). If running in parallel agents, use Agent Mail file reservations to
coordinate, or merge sequentially. Git should auto-merge cleanly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-03-20T22:25:22.689277647Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T02:56:26.069936773Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T02:56:07.477639286Z","close_reason":"Added mouse support: tea.WithMouseCellMotion() to dashboard/palette/tutorial, mouse wheel scrolling in dashboard, NTM_MOUSE=0 opt-out. All tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mouse","phase-1","quick-win","tui","upgrade"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-08fgf","depends_on_id":"br-7zdzv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:32:51.743591625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-08fgf","depends_on_id":"br-phdna","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:34:44.589725742Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-27cl0","title":"Phase 4b: Extend Harmonica Spring Animations to Focus, Progress, and Sparklines","description":"# Extend Harmonica Spring Animations to Focus, Progress, and Sparklines

## Background
Phase 1b wires Harmonica for toasts. This extends springs across ALL animation surfaces.
Estimate: 240m (4h). SpringManager ~100 LOC + 4 integrations ~30-50 LOC each + tests ~200 LOC.

## Animation Targets

### 1. Panel Focus Transition (requires Phase 3b focus ring)
  Focus indicator border opacity springs between 0 and 1.
  Spring: FPS(60), 10.0, 0.7 (fast, minimal overshoot)

### 2. Progress Bar Value Transitions
  displayPercent springs to targetPercent.
  Spring: FPS(60), 5.0, 0.3 (visible overshoot — satisfying bounce)
  DEAD ZONE: Only spring if |delta| > 2%. Prevents constant animation when values fluctuate
  slightly (e.g., context usage oscillating between 74% and 76%).

### 3. Sparkline Data Transitions
  Each value springs from previous to new.
  Spring: FPS(60), 8.0, 0.5 (moderate overshoot)

### 4. Scroll Position (with viewport from Phase 2c)
  Spring-animated momentum scrolling.
  Spring: FPS(60), 12.0, 0.6 (fast, slight deceleration)

## Spring Manager — NO MUTEX NEEDED
Bubble Tea is single-threaded (Update/View run on one goroutine). SpringManager doesn't
need sync.Mutex. Use a plain map:

  type SpringManager struct {
      springs map[string]*AnimatedFloat
  }
  type AnimatedFloat struct {
      Value, Velocity, Target float64
      Spring                  harmonica.Spring
      DeadZone                float64  // minimum delta to trigger animation
  }
  func (sm *SpringManager) Set(id string, target float64)
  func (sm *SpringManager) SetWithParams(id string, target, stiffness, damping float64)
  func (sm *SpringManager) Tick()
  func (sm *SpringManager) Get(id string) float64
  func (sm *SpringManager) IsAnimating() bool
  func (sm *SpringManager) IsSettled(id string) bool

## Tick Rate Management
  When IsAnimating() → 16ms tick. When settled → normal rate (100ms/500ms).
  Already partially solved by StateActive/StateIdle.

## ReducedMotion
  ALL springs: if ReducedMotionEnabled() { Value = Target, Velocity = 0 }

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)

### Test file: internal/tui/components/spring_manager_test.go (new)
  func TestSpringManagerSetAndGet(t *testing.T) {
      sm := NewSpringManager()
      sm.Set("test", 10.0)
      if sm.Get("test") != 0.0 { t.Error("initial value should be 0") }
      for i := 0; i < 200; i++ { sm.Tick() }
      if math.Abs(sm.Get("test")-10.0) > 0.5 { t.Errorf("not settled: %f", sm.Get("test")) }
  }

  func TestSpringManagerIsAnimating(t *testing.T) {
      sm := NewSpringManager()
      if sm.IsAnimating() { t.Error("empty should not animate") }
      sm.Set("test", 10.0)
      if !sm.IsAnimating() { t.Error("should be animating") }
      for i := 0; i < 300; i++ { sm.Tick() }
      if sm.IsAnimating() { t.Error("should be settled") }
  }

  func TestSpringManagerDeadZone(t *testing.T) {
      sm := NewSpringManager()
      sm.springs["test"] = &AnimatedFloat{Value: 50.0, Target: 50.0, DeadZone: 2.0,
          Spring: harmonica.NewSpring(harmonica.FPS(60), 5.0, 0.3)}
      sm.Set("test", 51.0) // delta = 1.0 < dead zone 2.0
      if sm.IsAnimating() { t.Error("delta within dead zone should not animate") }
      sm.Set("test", 53.0) // delta = 3.0 > dead zone 2.0
      if !sm.IsAnimating() { t.Error("delta above dead zone should animate") }
  }

  func TestSpringManagerReducedMotion(t *testing.T) {
      t.Setenv("NTM_REDUCE_MOTION", "1")
      sm := NewSpringManager()
      sm.Set("test", 10.0)
      if sm.Get("test") != 10.0 { t.Error("instant in reduced motion") }
      if sm.IsAnimating() { t.Error("no animation in reduced motion") }
  }

  func TestSpringManagerMultiple(t *testing.T) {
      sm := NewSpringManager()
      sm.Set("a", 5.0); sm.Set("b", 10.0); sm.Set("c", 15.0)
      for i := 0; i < 300; i++ { sm.Tick() }
      for id, target := range map[string]float64{"a": 5, "b": 10, "c": 15} {
          if math.Abs(sm.Get(id)-target) > 0.5 { t.Errorf("%s not settled", id) }
      }
  }

  func TestSpringManagerOvershoot(t *testing.T) {
      sm := NewSpringManager()
      sm.SetWithParams("test", 10.0, 5.0, 0.3)
      max := 0.0
      for i := 0; i < 100; i++ {
          sm.Tick()
          if sm.Get("test") > max { max = sm.Get("test") }
      }
      if max <= 10.0 { t.Error("underdamped should overshoot") }
  }

  func BenchmarkSpringManagerTick20(b *testing.B) {
      sm := NewSpringManager()
      for i := 0; i < 20; i++ { sm.Set(fmt.Sprintf("s%d", i), float64(i)*10) }
      b.ResetTimer()
      for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ { sm.Tick() }
  }

## E2E / VHS Verification
Video review critical: ntm-dashboard-navigate for focus transitions, progress overshoot.

## Logging
- Log spring creation: "spring: %s stiffness=%.1f damping=%.1f deadzone=%.1f"
- Debug log active count per tick: "springs: %d active"
- Log tick rate change: "tick: %dms (springs active=%v)"
- Log dead zone suppression: "spring %s: delta %.2f within dead zone %.2f, skipping"

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SpringManager created (NO mutex — single-threaded)
- [ ] Dead zone prevents micro-oscillation animation
- [ ] Focus transitions spring-animated
- [ ] Progress bars spring to new values
- [ ] Sparklines spring-animate data updates
- [ ] ReducedMotion instant
- [ ] Tick rate increases when springs active
- [ ] All 7 tests + benchmark pass
- [ ] Benchmark: < 500ns per Tick() for 20 springs
- [ ] Video shows smooth animations

## Files to Create
- internal/tui/components/spring_manager.go
- internal/tui/components/spring_manager_test.go

## Files to Modify
- internal/tui/dashboard/update.go (tick management)
- internal/tui/dashboard/focus.go (focus spring)
- internal/tui/components/progress.go (value spring)
- internal/tui/components/sparkline.go (data spring)

## Risk: MEDIUM — Multiple render paths. Dead zone and benchmark mitigate perf risk.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-20T22:31:14.684571247Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T00:51:34.190864654Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["animation","harmonica","phase-4","polish","tui","upgrade"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-27cl0","depends_on_id":"br-7dnb7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:33:59.460840152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-27cl0","depends_on_id":"br-h3z1b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:34:02.556497324Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-27cl0","depends_on_id":"br-phdna","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:34:56.571568722Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-27cl0","depends_on_id":"br-zqcqd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:34:04.365967797Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-3qs46","title":"Attention Feed Phase 1d3: Resolve bead_orphaned feasibility and honest unsupported-condition behavior","description":"# Why this bead exists
`bead_orphaned` is strategically useful but epistemically risky. ntm should not claim that work is orphaned unless it can defend that conclusion from real observable data. This bead exists to resolve that uncertainty deliberately instead of letting it hide inside a larger coordination bead.

# Scope
1. Determine whether `bead_orphaned` can be implemented honestly from ntm's real visibility into beads/bv/agent state.
2. If it can, define the exact observable criteria and signal payload.
3. If it cannot, define the exact unsupported / degraded behavior for commands, capabilities, and help output.
4. Ensure wait/digest/help/capabilities surfaces all tell the same truth about whether `bead_orphaned` is supported, partially supported, or unavailable.
5. Leave behind explicit rationale so future contributors know whether the limitation is conceptual, architectural, or just unimplemented.

# Design considerations
- This is the easiest coordination signal to fake badly.
- An honest `not supported` is better than a seductive but unreliable heuristic.
- The result of this bead should shape command behavior and documentation, not remain tribal knowledge.

# Likely touch points
- `internal/bv/`
- any beads integration surfaces in ntm
- wait/digest/capabilities/help output

# Detailed verification expectations
- Tests for supported and unsupported behavior, whichever outcome is chosen.
- If unsupported, golden tests for the exact capability/help/command behavior seen by operators.
- If supported, scenario tests that prove the orphaned conclusion follows from observable evidence.
- Detailed logs in tests that explain why the signal is supported, unsupported, or degraded.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `bead_orphaned` is either implemented honestly or marked unsupported honestly.
- [ ] The resulting truth is reflected consistently across runtime behavior and documentation.
- [ ] Future maintainers can tell why the chosen behavior exists.","notes":"Partial prep slice complete: exposed attention capabilities metadata with bead_orphaned explicitly marked unsupported in internal/robot/attention_contract.go and internal/robot/capabilities.go; added non-overlapping capabilities tests; left robot.go, snapshot-related robot tests, attention_feed.go, and attention_feed tests to their current owners.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:35.575801715Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T02:54:55.267867129Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention-feed","coordination","feasibility","phase-1","robot","signals"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3qs46","depends_on_id":"br-04qv0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:40.217514630Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -1221,7 +1221,7 @@
 {"id":"br-obaln","title":"Phase 3a: Decompose dashboard.go (7017 LOC) into Logical Sub-Files","description":"# Decompose dashboard.go (7017 LOC) into Logical Sub-Files

## Background
7,017-line monolith — classic anti-pattern. All files stay in package dashboard.
ZERO LOGIC CHANGE — every function moves verbatim.

## Estimate: 120 minutes
This is a mechanical file-split. Each step is: cut code, paste to new file, add imports,
run go build. The Go compiler catches issues instantly. 7017 LOC ÷ ~800 LOC/file = ~9 files.
At ~12 min per file (cut, paste, fix imports, build) = ~108 min. Rounded to 120m.

## Target File Structure
  dashboard/
  ├── model.go         (~400)  Model struct, New(), constants, PostQuitAction
  ├── messages.go      (~200)  All custom message types
  ├── init_cmd.go      (~300)  Init(), initial fetch commands
  ├── update.go        (~1400) Update(), keyboard handling
  ├── update_data.go   (~1100) Data update handlers (accept*Msg)
  ├── view.go          (~800)  View(), header, footer
  ├── view_panels.go   (~1500) Content section, panel grid
  ├── helpers.go       (~800)  Formatting, utilities
  ├── run.go           (~220)  Run(), RunPopup(), RunWithOptions()

## AGENTS.md Compliance: No File Deletion
Per AGENTS.md RULE NUMBER 1: "NEVER delete a file without express permission."
After all code is extracted, dashboard.go should be reduced to a package-level comment:
  // Package dashboard — see model.go, update.go, view.go for implementation.
  // This file intentionally left as a redirect after decomposition.
  package dashboard
Do NOT delete dashboard.go without explicit user permission.

## CRITICAL: Zero Logic Changes
Every function moves verbatim. No renaming, restructuring, or improvements.
git diff should show only file additions and dashboard.go deletions.

## Implementation Steps
1. Create model.go → move Model struct, New(), constants → go build
2. Create messages.go → move all *Msg types → go build
3. Create init_cmd.go → move Init() → go build
4. Continue for each file (build after each move)
5. Reduce dashboard.go to redirect comment
6. go test -short ./internal/tui/dashboard/...

## Unit Tests (verify no regression)

### Pre/post test comparison protocol
  # Before:
  go test -v -count=1 ./internal/tui/dashboard/... > /tmp/dash_before.log 2>&1
  # After:
  go test -v -count=1 ./internal/tui/dashboard/... > /tmp/dash_after.log 2>&1
  # Compare (only timestamps should differ):
  diff <(grep '^---' /tmp/dash_before.log) <(grep '^---' /tmp/dash_after.log)

### New structural test: internal/tui/dashboard/structure_test.go
  func TestModelStructExists(t *testing.T) { var m Model; _ = m }
  func TestNewReturnsValidModel(t *testing.T) {
      m := New("test", "/tmp/test")
      if m.session != "test" { t.Error("session not set") }
  }
  func TestRunFunctionExists(t *testing.T) {
      var f func(string, string) (*PostQuitAction, error) = Run
      _ = f
  }

## E2E / VHS Verification
VHS captures MUST be pixel-identical to Phase 2 baselines.
Run all 5 profiles. Any visual difference = bug in the split.

## Logging
- No runtime logging changes (structural refactor only)
- Log decomposition completion: file count, LOC per file

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] dashboard.go reduced to redirect comment (not deleted)
- [ ] All code in logical sub-files (9 new files)
- [ ] go build ./cmd/ntm succeeds
- [ ] go vet ./internal/tui/dashboard/... clean
- [ ] All existing tests pass with identical results
- [ ] structure_test.go passes
- [ ] VHS captures pixel-identical to Phase 2
- [ ] No import cycles

## Files to Create
model.go, messages.go, init_cmd.go, update.go, update_data.go, view.go,
view_panels.go, helpers.go, run.go

Structural verification tests go in existing helpers_test.go — no new test file.
(AGENTS.md: no file proliferation)

 {"id":"br-ofwdn","title":"Phase 1c: Replace Custom Spinner with bubbles/spinner","description":"# Replace Custom Spinner with bubbles/spinner

## Background
internal/tui/components/spinner.go (130 LOC) defines a custom Spinner struct (NOT SpinnerModel)
with Init(), Update(), View(), and TickCmd() methods. It has frame sets, color, gradient support,
and respects AnimationsEnabled().

## ACTUAL Current API (verified from source)
  type Spinner struct {
      Style          SpinnerStyle
      Color          lipgloss.Color
      Frame          int
      FPS            time.Duration
      Label          string
      Gradient       bool
      GradientColors []string
  }
  func NewSpinner() Spinner
  func (s Spinner) Init() tea.Cmd
  func (s Spinner) Update(msg tea.Msg) (Spinner, tea.Cmd)
  func (s Spinner) View() string
  func (s Spinner) TickCmd() tea.Cmd

Used in: dashboard.go, layout.go, components_test.go
AnimationsEnabled() checked at: spinner.go lines 74, 84, 119

## What bubbles/spinner Provides
  type Model struct { Spinner Spinner; Style lipgloss.Style }
  func New(opts ...Option) Model
  func (m Model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (Model, tea.Cmd)
  func (m Model) View() string
  func (m Model) Tick() tea.Cmd  // returns TickMsg

## Implementation Plan — PRESERVE EXACT API SURFACE

### 1. Keep Spinner struct, change internals
The Spinner struct must keep its EXACT field names and method signatures because dashboard.go
and layout.go reference them directly. Internal implementation changes to delegate to
bubbles/spinner.

### 2. Key change: store bubbles spinner.Model internally
  type Spinner struct {
      inner          bspinner.Model  // bubbles/spinner
      Style          SpinnerStyle    // preserved field
      Color          lipgloss.Color  // preserved field
      Label          string          // preserved field
      Gradient       bool            // preserved field
      GradientColors []string        // preserved field
      // Frame and FPS no longer needed — bubbles handles internally
  }

### 3. Map SpinnerStyle to bubbles presets
  func mapStyle(s SpinnerStyle) bspinner.Spinner {
      switch s {
      case SpinnerDots: return bspinner.Dot
      case SpinnerLine: return bspinner.Line
      // ... etc
      }
  }

### 4. View() delegates to inner.View() with gradient overlay
  func (s Spinner) View() string {
      if !AnimationsEnabled() { return staticChar(s.Style) }
      base := s.inner.View()
      if s.Gradient { return applyGradient(base, s.GradientColors) }
      return base
  }

### 5. Update() delegates to inner.Update()
### 6. TickCmd() wraps inner.Tick()

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)

### Expand: internal/tui/components/components_test.go (spinner section)
  func TestSpinnerAllStyles(t *testing.T) {
      // Verify NewSpinner() produces valid spinner for each style
      for _, style := range allSpinnerStyles() {
          s := NewSpinner()
          s.Style = style
          view := s.View()
          if view == "" { t.Errorf("style %d empty view", style) }
      }
  }

  func TestSpinnerReducedMotion(t *testing.T) {
      t.Setenv("NTM_ANIMATIONS", "0")
      s := NewSpinner()
      v1 := s.View()
      s, _ = s.Update(s.TickCmd())
      v2 := s.View()
      // Reduced motion: view should not change between ticks
  }

  func TestSpinnerGradient(t *testing.T) {
      s := NewSpinner()
      s.Gradient = true
      s.GradientColors = []string{"#ff0000", "#00ff00"}
      view := s.View()
      if view == "" { t.Error("gradient spinner empty") }
  }

  func TestSpinnerTickCmd(t *testing.T) {
      s := NewSpinner()
      cmd := s.TickCmd()
      if cmd == nil { t.Error("TickCmd should return non-nil") }
  }

  func BenchmarkSpinnerView(b *testing.B) {
      s := NewSpinner()
      b.ResetTimer()
      for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ { _ = s.View() }
  }

## E2E / VHS Verification
Spinners appear in dashboard loading states. Run ntm-dashboard-empty capture.

## Logging
- Debug log style mapping: "spinner: style %d -> bubbles %v"
- Debug log when AnimationsEnabled() returns false

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Spinner struct keeps exact same public API (fields + methods)
- [ ] All existing code in dashboard.go/layout.go compiles without changes
- [ ] All SpinnerStyle values map to valid bubbles presets
- [ ] Gradient overlay still works
- [ ] AnimationsEnabled() check preserved
- [ ] All existing spinner tests in components_test.go pass
- [ ] New tests pass
- [ ] VHS captures show correct spinner animation

## Files to Modify
- internal/tui/components/spinner.go (rewrite internals, keep API)

 {"id":"br-phdna","title":"EPIC: TUI Glamour Upgrade — Leverage Full Charmbracelet Ecosystem","description":"# TUI Glamour Upgrade Epic

## Executive Summary
NTM's TUI (49,213 LOC) is a sophisticated Bubble Tea application with 22 dashboard panels,
3 Catppuccin themes, responsive 5-tier layout, and custom animations. However, it dramatically
underutilizes the Charmbracelet ecosystem it depends on. The `harmonica` spring physics library
is in go.mod with ZERO imports. `bubbles/table`, `bubbles/viewport`, `bubbles/progress`,
`bubbles/spinner`, `bubbles/help`, and `bubbles/list` are all available but replaced by
hand-rolled alternatives. Mouse support is never enabled. The `huh` forms library is absent.

This epic systematically upgrades the TUI to leverage these battle-tested components, adds
spring-based physics animations via Harmonica, enables mouse interaction, and decomposes the
7,017-line dashboard.go monolith — all verified at every step via the TUI Inspector VHS
capture-and-compare loop.

## Architecture Context
- Entry: `cmd/ntm/main.go` → `cli.Execute()` → Cobra commands
- Dashboard: `internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go` (7,017 LOC monolith)
- Components: `internal/tui/components/` (15 files, ~3,300 LOC)
- Styling: `internal/tui/styles/` + `internal/tui/theme/` (~3,000 LOC)
- Layout: `internal/tui/layout/layout.go` (5 responsive tiers with hysteresis)
- Panels: `internal/tui/dashboard/panels/` (22 implementations)
- TUI programs: dashboard, palette (cli/palette.go), tutorial (cli/tutorial.go), init_wizard

## Key Dependencies Already in go.mod
- charmbracelet/bubbletea v1.3.10
- charmbracelet/bubbles v1.0.0 (table, list, viewport, progress, spinner, help — all unused)
- charmbracelet/lipgloss v1.1.1-0.20250404203927-76690c660834
- charmbracelet/harmonica v0.2.0 (ZERO imports anywhere)
- charmbracelet/glamour v0.10.0
- charmbracelet/x/ansi v0.11.6

## Verification Infrastructure
- VHS installed at /home/ubuntu/go/bin/vhs
- 5 NTM-specific TUI Inspector profiles: ntm-dashboard-empty, ntm-dashboard-help,
  ntm-dashboard-navigate, ntm-dashboard-wide, ntm-palette
- Capture script: /dp/tui_inspector/scripts/capture_ntm_tui.sh
- Suite runner: /dp/tui_inspector/scripts/run_ntm_tui_suite.sh
- 5 native VHS tapes in testdata/vhs/ for unit-level visual regression

## Overarching Goals
1. Replace hand-rolled components with battle-tested Bubbles equivalents
2. Add spring-based physics animations via Harmonica (already a dependency!)
3. Enable mouse interaction for modern terminal UX
4. Decompose the dashboard.go monolith for maintainability
5. Add Huh forms for interactive workflows
6. Verify every change via TUI Inspector capture → compare → iterate loop
7. Maintain zero regressions in existing functionality","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-20T22:24:27.002836528Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-20T22:24:27.002836528Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmbracelet","epic","tui","upgrade"]}
-{"id":"br-pihrr","title":"Phase 2b: Add bubbles/table to History and Timeline Panels","description":"# Add bubbles/table to History and Timeline Panels

## Background
panels/history.go (862 LOC) renders tabular data with manual string formatting AND has
3 filter systems: historyStatusFilter (all/ok/error), historyAgentFilter (all/claude/codex/
gemini), historyTimeFilter (all/1h/24h/7d). It also has cursor management, replay on Enter,
and keybindings for filter cycling.

panels/timeline.go (1,111 LOC) similarly renders events with agent colors.

bubbles/table provides column alignment, scrolling, selection, and mouse support — but we
must PRESERVE the existing filter systems and replay functionality.

## CRITICAL: Existing Filter Architecture
  historyStatusFilter: Cycle with 'f' key → all/ok/error
  historyAgentFilter: Cycle with 'a' key → all/claude/codex/gemini
  historyTimeFilter: Cycle with 't' key → all/1h/24h/7d
  applyFilters(): Combines all 3 filters → produces filtered entry list
  moveCursor(): Manages selection within filtered results

bubbles/table does NOT have built-in filtering. The filter logic STAYS in the panel.
Only the RENDERING of filtered results moves to bubbles/table.

## Implementation Plan — History Panel

### 1. Add table.Model to HistoryPanel struct
  type HistoryPanel struct {
      PanelBase
      table    table.Model        // NEW: replaces inline rendering
      entries  []history.Entry    // raw data
      filtered []history.Entry    // after filters applied
      // ... existing filter fields preserved
  }

### 2. Define columns (adaptive to tier)
  func historyColumns(width int) []table.Column {
      if width < 120 { return narrowColumns() }
      if width < 200 { return splitColumns() }
      return wideColumns()
  }

### 3. Convert filtered entries → table.Row on each filter change
  func (m *HistoryPanel) rebuildTableRows() {
      rows := make([]table.Row, len(m.filtered))
      for i, e := range m.filtered {
          rows[i] = table.Row{formatTime(e.Timestamp), e.Command, e.Target, statusBadge(e.Status)}
      }
      m.table.SetRows(rows)
  }

### 4. Filter cycling preserved — after filter change, call rebuildTableRows()
### 5. Replay on Enter — get selected row index, map back to filtered[index]
### 6. Style with Catppuccin theme
### 7. Route table.Update(msg) when panel focused

## Timeline Panel — Similar approach
  Columns: Time, Event Type, Agent, Description
  Agent column uses AgentBadge for color coding
  No filter system (simpler than history)

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)

### Expand existing: panels/history_test.go (634 LOC)
  func TestHistoryTableColumns(t *testing.T) {
      p := NewHistoryPanel()
      p.SetSize(120, 20)
      p.SetEntries(testHistoryEntries(10), nil)
      view := p.View()
      if !strings.Contains(view, "Time") { t.Error("missing Time header") }
      if !strings.Contains(view, "Command") { t.Error("missing Command header") }
  }

  func TestHistoryTableNavigation(t *testing.T) {
      p := NewHistoryPanel()
      p.SetSize(120, 20)
      p.SetEntries(testHistoryEntries(5), nil)
      p.Focus()
      p.Update(tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyDown})
      sel, ok := p.selectedEntry()
      if !ok { t.Error("should have selection after down") }
      _ = sel
  }

  func TestHistoryTableFilterPreserved(t *testing.T) {
      p := NewHistoryPanel()
      p.SetSize(120, 20)
      p.SetEntries(testHistoryEntries(10), nil) // mix of ok and error
      // Apply status filter
      p.Update(tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyRunes, Runes: []rune{'f'}}) // cycle filter
      // Verify table rows reflect filter
      // Table should have fewer rows than total entries
  }

  func TestHistoryTableTierAdaptation(t *testing.T) {
      p := NewHistoryPanel()
      p.SetSize(100, 20) // Narrow
      view := p.View()
      // Narrow columns should be used
      p.SetSize(250, 20) // Wide
      view = p.View()
      // Wide columns should include Target
  }

  func TestHistoryTableEmptyState(t *testing.T) {
      p := NewHistoryPanel()
      p.SetSize(120, 20)
      p.SetEntries(nil, nil)
      view := p.View()
      if view == "" { t.Error("empty state should render") }
  }

  func TestHistoryTableScrolling(t *testing.T) {
      p := NewHistoryPanel()
      p.SetSize(120, 10)
      p.SetEntries(testHistoryEntries(50), nil)
      p.Focus()
      for i := 0; i < 20; i++ { p.Update(tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyDown}) }
  }

### Expand existing: panels/timeline_test.go (1039 LOC)
  func TestTimelineTableColumns(t *testing.T) { ... }
  func TestTimelineTableAgentBadge(t *testing.T) { ... }

### Benchmark
  func BenchmarkHistoryTableView(b *testing.B) {
      p := NewHistoryPanel()
      p.SetSize(120, 20)
      p.SetEntries(testHistoryEntries(100), nil)
      b.ResetTimer()
      for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ { _ = p.View() }
  }

## E2E / VHS Verification
Create testdata/vhs/dashboard-table-scroll.tape. Tab to history panel, arrow down, PgDown.
Run TUI Inspector ntm-dashboard-navigate profile.

## Logging
- Log table rebuild: "history table: %d rows (filtered from %d)"
- Log column adaptation: "history table: %d cols for width=%d"
- Debug log filter changes: "history: filter %s -> %s"

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] History panel uses bubbles/table for rendering
- [ ] 3 filter systems (status/agent/time) preserved and functional
- [ ] Filter cycling ('f'/'a'/'t' keys) works and rebuilds table rows
- [ ] Replay on Enter works via filtered entry index
- [ ] Column widths adapt to layout tier
- [ ] Empty state renders gracefully
- [ ] Catppuccin styling on header/selected/cells
- [ ] Timeline panel uses bubbles/table with AgentBadge column
- [ ] All existing history_test.go (634 LOC) tests pass
- [ ] All existing timeline_test.go (1039 LOC) tests pass
- [ ] 6+ new tests pass per panel
- [ ] VHS shows proper column alignment and scrolling

## Files to Modify
- internal/tui/dashboard/panels/history.go (add table.Model, rebuildTableRows)
- internal/tui/dashboard/panels/timeline.go (add table.Model)

## Files to Create
None — tests go in existing history_test.go and timeline_test.go

## Risk: MEDIUM — Filter-to-table data flow is the key complexity. Replay must correctly
map table selection index back to the filtered entry.  {"id":"br-rb9oj","title":"Overlay-Feed T9: Integration tests and Go e2e scenarios with artifact logging for overlay-feed interaction","description":"# Why this bead exists
Once the attention feed is operational and the overlay integration beads are implemented, we need integration and end-to-end verification that proves the human/agent handoff loop actually works. The earlier version of this bead planned shell-script e2e drivers, which is a poor fit for this Go-only repo. This bead now standardizes on Go-based integration and e2e harnesses with detailed artifact logging.

# Scope
1. Add in-process Go integration tests for overlay/feed interaction, including:
   - attention panel data flow and truncation
   - badge state transitions
   - cursor propagation through zoom/dismiss continuity paths
   - attention-cursor startup/focus behavior
   - graceful degradation when feed visibility is unavailable
2. Add Go-based e2e scenarios under `e2e/` that drive real tmux sessions for:
   - overlay launch and dismiss
   - auto-popup detection
   - overlay binding setup and cleanup
   - zoom-to-pane with cursor hint propagation
   - `assign --watch` overlay discoverability
   - robot-initiated overlay handoff once available
3. Make the Go e2e harness idempotent and self-cleaning so it creates and tears down its own tmux sessions/resources.
4. Ensure all scenarios use the shared Go scenario/artifact harness instead of inventing separate fixture and logging conventions.
5. Ensure all scenarios produce detailed, timestamped artifacts in predictable locations inside the repo's test artifact structure or an explicitly documented temporary artifact root.
6. Keep graceful degradation scenarios first-class rather than treating them as an afterthought.
7. Use Go test code as the canonical harness. Auxiliary debugging helpers are fine, but not shell-script primary runners.

# Detailed verification expectations
- Subtest-based integration coverage with `t.Logf` diagnostics for state transitions, cursor propagation, and rendered output.
- Go e2e scenarios with timeout protection, explicit cleanup, and artifact capture for stdout/stderr, key state transitions, and session lifecycle.
- Artifact logs should include timestamps, session identifiers, cursor values, focus targets, and degraded-mode markers where relevant.
- Failures should report enough structured context that a maintainer can understand what broke without immediately reproducing it interactively.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Integration coverage exists for the key overlay/feed interaction paths and graceful degradation.
- [ ] Go-based e2e scenarios exist for the main overlay/feed workflows and clean up their own resources.
- [ ] The scenarios reuse the shared Go scenario/artifact harness rather than duplicating fixture plumbing.
- [ ] Detailed timestamped artifacts are produced in predictable locations.
- [ ] The primary verification harness is implemented in Go rather than shell scripts.
- [ ] The bead preserves all previously planned happy-path and degraded-path coverage.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:14:52.872086749Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T01:25:35.949568520Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention-feed","e2e","overlay","phase-3","tests","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-rb9oj","depends_on_id":"br-5u235","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:16:08.534278552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rb9oj","depends_on_id":"br-7746h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:25:31.544052650Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rb9oj","depends_on_id":"br-7h8it","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:25:31.803751872Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rb9oj","depends_on_id":"br-a6cmp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:16:13.562886901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rb9oj","depends_on_id":"br-abp0s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:16:10.690138392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rb9oj","depends_on_id":"br-ht8jy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:16:11.494253800Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rb9oj","depends_on_id":"br-la5zy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:16:12.350680821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rb9oj","depends_on_id":"br-ut0d8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:16:09.720207583Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rb9oj","depends_on_id":"br-x66gc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:16:14.447924217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rb9oj","depends_on_id":"br-xq4b0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:16:07.739874585Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-rkh17","title":"Attention Feed Phase 4a1: Document the operator loop in robot help and capabilities output","description":"# Why this bead exists
Help and capabilities output are the canonical contract surface for both human users and agent drivers. They should not be bundled with dashboard rendering concerns. This bead isolates the machine-readable and CLI-discoverability side of the work.

# Scope
1. Update `--robot-help` and `--robot-capabilities` so the attention-feed command cluster, condition taxonomy, cursor/resync behavior, and profile/preset system are explicitly documented.
2. Include concrete examples for the canonical operator loop: snapshot bootstrap, steady-state attention/digest, raw event replay, and expired-cursor resync.
3. Make unsupported or partially observable conditions explicit in capabilities output, especially for `bead_orphaned` if it remains unavailable.
4. Ensure examples and schema notes are accurate enough that future users do not need a separate markdown plan document.
5. Keep the output aligned with actual implementation rather than aspirational language.
6. Expose a machine-readable support matrix covering commands, conditions, profiles, transport features, degraded/unsupported reasons, and any schema/capability version markers needed by agent clients.
7. Document cursor-retention and transport-liveness expectations strongly enough that long-lived clients can adapt without trial and error.

# Design considerations
- Help/capabilities are part of the product surface, not an afterthought.
- Capability output should be precise enough for an agent to adapt automatically.
- The text should emphasize the one-obvious-way operator loop rather than listing an undifferentiated pile of commands.
- Machine-readable capability output should tell agents not just what exists, but which features are supported, degraded, or intentionally unavailable and why.

# Likely touch points
- `internal/cli/root.go`
- capability/help generation in robot-mode code
- `internal/output/`

# Detailed verification expectations
- Golden tests for help/capabilities output.
- Tests proving the documented conditions, profiles, commands, schema-version markers, and transport-feature support matrix match implementation reality.
- Diff-friendly diagnostics when examples or capability flags drift.
- Scenario checks that help/capabilities mention snapshot bootstrap, resync, retention hints, and transport-liveness expectations explicitly.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Help and capabilities output accurately teach the operator loop.
- [ ] Conditions, profiles, transport features, and unsupported/degraded behavior are represented honestly.
- [ ] Capability output is machine-readable enough for agent clients to adapt automatically.
- [ ] The contract is discoverable from ntm itself without external documents.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:21.584305811Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T01:25:34.736588130Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention-feed","dashboard","docs","phase-4","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-rkh17","depends_on_id":"br-6tzh9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:27.565570681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rkh17","depends_on_id":"br-7kigk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:27.950976700Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rkh17","depends_on_id":"br-91gti","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:11:28.696168421Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rkh17","depends_on_id":"br-kpvhy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:26.873210361Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rkh17","depends_on_id":"br-slg9g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:57:17.501881264Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rkh17","depends_on_id":"br-t540i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:28.315242931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-rkh17","depends_on_id":"br-tsqxo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:27.204026005Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-slg9g","title":"Attention Feed Phase 2a2: Add snapshot attention summary and bootstrap ergonomics","description":"# Why this bead exists
Once snapshot bootstrap correctness exists, snapshot still needs to be genuinely useful to operators. A raw cold-start dump with a cursor is functional, but not ergonomic. This bead adds the snapshot-level attention summary and bootstrap UX polish that helps a frontier LLM orient quickly without turning snapshot into a second digest implementation.

# Scope
1. Add a compact snapshot-level attention summary aligned with the same actionability/severity taxonomy used by the feed and digest surfaces.
2. Surface top attention items or compact counts in a way that helps an operator choose the next command without rereading the entire snapshot.
3. Ensure any snapshot summary stays consistent with the real signal set: core signals, mail signals, conflict signals, and honest unsupported-condition behavior.
4. Add resync guidance and next-step hints that are mechanical rather than speculative.
5. Keep snapshot summary logic shallow enough that digest remains the canonical steady-state delta surface.

# Design considerations
- Snapshot should help an operator orient, not replace the digest.
- Summary counts and hints must be generated from the same underlying signal taxonomy so snapshot/digest do not drift apart.
- Unsupported or partially observable conditions must be represented honestly, not smoothed over.

# Likely touch points
- `internal/robot/robot.go`
- snapshot-producing helpers
- `internal/output/`
- help/capabilities examples that describe bootstrap flow

# Detailed verification expectations
- Golden tests for snapshot responses that include attention summary fields.
- Tests that compare snapshot summary buckets against the underlying signal state for consistency.
- Transcript scenarios showing a realistic cold-start operator loop using the summary to choose the next targeted command.
- Detailed logs in tests for summary bucket counts, unsupported-condition markers, and next-step hints.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Snapshot surfaces a compact operator-oriented summary without duplicating digest semantics.
- [ ] Snapshot summary is derived from the same real signal taxonomy as the rest of the attention-feed system.
- [ ] Unsupported or partial conditions are represented honestly.
- [ ] Bootstrap ergonomics are materially better for users while preserving the cold-start contract.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":150,"created_at":"2026-03-21T00:57:12.030218053Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:39.046562188Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention-feed","bootstrap","cli","phase-2","robot","snapshot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-slg9g","depends_on_id":"br-04qv0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:37.920524355Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-slg9g","depends_on_id":"br-3qs46","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:39.045717977Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-slg9g","depends_on_id":"br-89wvt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:37.548558448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-slg9g","depends_on_id":"br-fsoxa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:38.287606898Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-slg9g","depends_on_id":"br-vdfjr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:38.660610688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
